contract:
  schema_version: 1
  scope:
    question: Which concrete claims, observables, and regime restrictions do the four anchor papers actually establish for DSSYK and its candidate de Sitter bulk descriptions?
    in_scope:
      - Extract the positive claim, the matched observable, and the regime or sector restriction for each anchor paper.
      - Record whether each paper speaks about DSSYK proper, a doubled constrained sector, or an auxiliary system.
  context_intake:
    must_read_refs:
      - Ref-2209-09997
      - Ref-2310-16994
      - Ref-2402-02584
      - Ref-2404-03535
    must_include_prior_outputs: []
  claims:
    - id: claim-anchor-ledger
      statement: The phase produces a paper-by-paper ledger of the decisive bulk-dual claims, observables, and regime restrictions across the four anchor papers.
      deliverables:
        - deliv-anchor-ledger
        - deliv-benchmark-observables
      acceptance_tests:
        - test-anchor-coverage
        - test-regime-explicitness
      references:
        - Ref-2209-09997
        - Ref-2310-16994
        - Ref-2402-02584
        - Ref-2404-03535
  deliverables:
    - id: deliv-anchor-ledger
      kind: derivation
      path: GPD/phases/01-anchor-audit-and-decision-criteria/anchor-ledger.md
      description: Ledger of the exact claims, sectors, and caveats extracted from the four anchor papers.
    - id: deliv-benchmark-observables
      kind: derivation
      path: GPD/phases/01-anchor-audit-and-decision-criteria/benchmark-observables.md
      description: Short ledger of which boundary observables are actually matched in each anchor paper.
  references:
    - id: Ref-2209-09997
      kind: paper
      locator: Adel A. Rahman, dS JT Gravity and Double-Scaled SYK, arXiv:2209.09997
      role: must_consider
      why_it_matters: Early positive-cosmological-constant JT proposal for a high-temperature DSSYK limit.
      applies_to:
        - claim-anchor-ledger
      must_surface: true
      required_actions:
        - read
        - compare
        - cite
    - id: Ref-2310-16994
      kind: paper
      locator: Vladimir Narovlansky and Herman Verlinde, Double-scaled SYK and de Sitter Holography, arXiv:2310.16994
      role: benchmark
      why_it_matters: Core doubled-SYK correlator match to a scalar Green function in de Sitter.
      applies_to:
        - claim-anchor-ledger
      must_surface: true
      required_actions:
        - read
        - compare
        - cite
    - id: Ref-2402-02584
      kind: paper
      locator: Herman Verlinde and Mengyang Zhang, SYK Correlators from 2D Liouville-de Sitter Gravity, arXiv:2402.02584
      role: benchmark
      why_it_matters: Strongest exact two-point-function claim in a Liouville-de Sitter setting.
      applies_to:
        - claim-anchor-ledger
      must_surface: true
      required_actions:
        - read
        - compare
        - cite
    - id: Ref-2404-03535
      kind: paper
      locator: Andreas Blommaert, Thomas G. Mertens, and Jacopo Papalini, The dilaton gravity hologram of double-scaled SYK, arXiv:2404.03535
      role: must_consider
      why_it_matters: Sharpens the bulk interpretation while exposing the fake-temperature caveat.
      applies_to:
        - claim-anchor-ledger
      must_surface: true
      required_actions:
        - read
        - compare
        - cite
  acceptance_tests:
    - id: test-anchor-coverage
      subject: claim-anchor-ledger
      kind: benchmark
      procedure: Confirm that all four anchor papers appear in the ledger with an explicit matched observable and an explicit sector description.
      pass_condition: No anchor paper is summarized only qualitatively; each one has a concrete observable and sector entry.
      evidence_required:
        - deliv-anchor-ledger
        - deliv-benchmark-observables
    - id: test-regime-explicitness
      subject: claim-anchor-ledger
      kind: consistency
      procedure: Confirm that temperature, doubled-sector, auxiliary-system, or exactness caveats are recorded whenever they matter for the bulk-dual claim.
      pass_condition: The ledger makes the regime or sector restriction explicit for each positive paper.
      evidence_required:
        - deliv-anchor-ledger
        - Ref-2310-16994
        - Ref-2402-02584
        - Ref-2404-03535
  forbidden_proxies:
    - id: fp-de-sitter-rhetoric
      subject: claim-anchor-ledger
      proxy: Treating generic de Sitter or holography language as equivalent to a demonstrated DSSYK bulk-dual observable match.
      reason: The report question is about a semiclassical bulk dual, so each anchor must be tied to an actual observable and regime.
  uncertainty_markers:
    weakest_anchors:
      - Whether the doubled equal-energy construction should count as DSSYK proper for the bulk-dual question.
    disconfirming_observations:
      - An anchor's positive claim collapses once its regime restriction is made explicit.
---

<objective>Extract the anchor-paper claims, matched observables, and regime restrictions before any stronger bulk-dual verdict is attempted.</objective>

<execution_context>Use the approved project contract and existing first-pass report as context, but force each positive statement back to a concrete anchor-paper claim and explicit regime.</execution_context>

<tasks>
  <task type="auto">
    <name>Task 1: Build the anchor claim ledger</name>
    <files>GPD/phases/01-anchor-audit-and-decision-criteria/anchor-ledger.md</files>
    <action>Record, for each anchor paper, the claimed bulk description, the matched DSSYK-side observable, and the explicit regime or sector restriction.</action>
    <verify>Check that every row names a paper, a concrete observable, and at least one caveat or validity condition when applicable.</verify>
    <done>The ledger covers all four anchors without collapsing doubled, auxiliary, and DSSYK-proper statements into one category.</done>
  </task>
  <task type="auto">
    <name>Task 2: Extract the decisive observables</name>
    <files>GPD/phases/01-anchor-audit-and-decision-criteria/benchmark-observables.md</files>
    <action>List which papers claim two-point, thermal, entropic, or operator-dictionary control and which do not.</action>
    <verify>Cross-check the list against the first-pass report and the project contract's observables.</verify>
    <done>The project has a compact observable ledger that can be reused by the next plan without ambiguity.</done>
  </task>
</tasks>

<verification>Reject any summary that does not distinguish DSSYK proper from doubled or auxiliary constructions, or that fails to name the actual observable being matched.</verification>

<success_criteria>The anchor ledger and observable ledger together let the project state, paper by paper, what was matched and under what restrictions.</success_criteria>
